truth.papers.A76.title: Microstructural Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Nb-Containing Medium-Mn Low-Density Steels
truth.papers.A76.paper_scope: Investigating the effect of Nb microalloying (0.02 and 0.04 wt% Nb) on microstructure evolution, nano-scale precipitation (NbC, κ-carbide), and mechanical properties of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C medium-Mn low-density steels, with quantitative strengthening analysis and thermodynamic/DFT calculations.

prediction.papers.A76.title: Microstructural Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Nb-Containing Medium-Mn Low-Density Steels
prediction.papers.A76.paper_scope: Investigate nano-scale precipitate evolution and mechanical properties of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C-(0.02/0.04)Nb medium-Mn low-density steels, focusing on NbC and kappa-carbide precipitation, austenite grain refinement, and strengthening mechanisms.

truth.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.descript: Preparation of steel strips by centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification: induction melting in water-cooled copper crucible, re-melting and pouring into rotating copper mold to obtain strips of 60×70×2.5 mm.

prediction.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.descript: Aging at 823 K for 3 h after centrifugal casting.
prediction.processes.proc_centrifugal_cast.processe: Ingot prepared by induction melting in a water-cooled copper crucible, re-melted and poured into a rotating copper mold; strip dimensions 60 mm × 70 mm × 2.5 mm.

truth.structures.sample_002nb_as_cast.overall_: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure, with irregular-shaped δ-ferrite dispersed in γ-austenite matrix.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_as_cast.microstr: 0.02Nb steel as-cast austenite grain size not directly measured, but Nb addition expected to refine grains.
Submicron white particles within austenite matrix (identified by TEM).
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_723K_3h.ove: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC particles and intragranular κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈16.4 vol%.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_723K_3h.mic: 0.02Nb steel: austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb variant. NbC particles refine grain.
Slightly coarsened submicron particles in austenite.
Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_773K_3h.ove: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈17.1 vol%.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_773K_3h.mic: Austenite grain size remains larger than 0.04Nb counterpart.
Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected. Larger than those in 0.04Nb steel.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_823K_3h.ove: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure with NbC and κ-carbides; ferrite content ≈18.3 vol%.
truth.structures.sample_002nb_aged_823K_3h.mic: Austenite grain size larger than 0.04Nb variant.
Intragranular, no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides detected.
truth.structures.sample_004nb_as_cast.overall_: Ferrite-austenite dual-phase structure; irregular-shaped δ-ferrite dispersed in austenite matrix.
truth.structures.sample_004nb_as_cast.microstr: Austenite grain size about half that of 0.02Nb steel due to NbC precipitation.
Submicron white particles in austenite (TEM identified).

Intragranular, consistently smaller than in 0.02Nb steel; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides.

truth.structures.sample_004nb_aged_773K_3h.mic: Grain refined by NbC, about half that of 0.02Nb steel.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.potential_mo: PAW-PBE with U scheme (U=2.59 eV for Fe), Gaussian smearing 0.05 eV
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: DFT calculation used to evaluate the formation energy of κ-carbide and the effect of Nb addition on it.
truth.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Thermodynamic calculations performed to explain precipitation behavior of κ-carbide and NbC; details of software not specified.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Spin-polarized density functional theory (DFT) within the generalized gradient approximation (GGA) using the Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE) functional
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.potential_mo: Projector augmented-wave (PAW) potentials; plane-wave kinetic energy cutoff 500 eV; Gaussian smearing width 0.05 eV; Hubbard U scheme with U = 2.59 eV for Fe atoms; vacuum spacing 15 Å; Brillouin zone integration with 6×6×6 Monkhorst-Pack k-point mesh; geometry optimization convergence criterion 0.03 eV Å⁻¹

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The yield strength of 0.04Nb steels exceeds that of 0.02Nb steels by ≈71 MPa, attributed to additional fine grain strengthening and dislocation strengthening despite a slight reduction in κ-carbide precipitation strengthening.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Austenite grain size of 0.04Nb steels is approximately half that of 0.02Nb steels due to NbC precipitation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Nb addition elevates the formation energy of κ-carbide, impeding its growth and coarsening; no coarsened intergranular κ-carbides were detected in either steel after aging at 723–823 K.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Ferrite content is not significantly affected by aging temperature or Nb content: as-cast 0.02Nb ~16.2 vol%, aged up to 18.3 vol% at 823 K; 0.04Nb similar.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: XRD patterns show no significant change in austenite/ferrite peak intensities with aging.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Microhardness and tensile properties were tested but quantitative values (except the 71 MPa difference) are not provided in the available text; further mechanical data may be present in the full paper.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: NbC particles exist as submicron white particles within the austenite matrix; their size and volume fraction increase slightly with higher aging temperature and Nb content according to Figure 4 statistics (numerical values not extracted from image).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The paper includes Figure 1 (XRD), Figure 2 (SEM), Figure 3 (TEM of NbC), Figure 4 (NbC statistics), and Figure 5 (EBSD maps); details from images are not fully captured as pure text mode.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Strain rate for tensile tests was 2.5 × 10^-4 s^-1; specimens had gauge width 4 mm and gauge length 12 mm; each test repeated at least four times.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Aging treatments were performed at 723, 773, 823 K for 3 h in a tubular vacuum furnace; the appropriate aging period for further investigation was selected but not explicitly stated in the provided text.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Electropolishing parameters: 10 vol% perchloric acid alcohol solution, 25 V, 10 s for SEM; TEM sample preparation: 10% perchloric acid + 90% alcohol, -30 °C, 35 V.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The yield strength of 0.04Nb steels exceeds that of 0.02Nb steels by approximately 71 MPa, mainly attributed to additional fine grain strengthening and dislocation strengthening, despite a slight reduction in precipitation strengthening of kappa-carbides with higher Nb content.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Quantitative strengthening mechanism analysis is discussed, but detailed absolute strength contributions for each mechanism are not explicitly tabulated in the provided text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: The austenite grain size of 0.04Nb steels is approximately half that of 0.02Nb steels due to NbC precipitation; absolute grain size values are not provided in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The study mentions investigation of structure evolution and mechanical properties under different aging periods, but specific data beyond the 3 h aging treatment are not included in the provided text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Absolute Vickers microhardness values, yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, and elongation values were measured but not explicitly reported in the provided text segment.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Exact NbC particle sizes, kappa-carbide sizes, and quantitative volume fraction numerical values are referenced in Figure 4 but not stated in the provided text.
